name: Mediterranean Seafood Casserole
recipeIngredient: 1 can (14 1/2 oz) stewed tomatoes, drained (reserve liquid)
2 small zucchini (about 6 oz each), cut into 1-inch chunks
2 cups Idaho Potato Cram Sauce (see preceding recipe)
recipeInstructions:
1. Very thinly slice one Idaho potato. In a large skillet filled with 1 inch of boiling water, cook the sliced potato until crisp tender, about 5 minutes. Drain and reserve the potato.
2. Meanwhile, cut the remaining 2 potatoes into 3/4-inch chunks. Wipe dry the skillet.
3. In the same skillet, combine the potato chunks, garlic, onion, bell pepper, drained stewed tomato liquid, bay leaf, basil, and thyme. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer, covered for 10 minutes. Stir in mushrooms, zucchini, drained stewed tomatoes, and 4 tablespoons chopped parsley. Cover and simmer for 6 minutes. Remove and discard the bay leaf.
4. Preheat the oven to 350°F. Spoon the mixture into a 3-quart shallow casserole dish. Stir in fish, then pour over Idaho Potato Cram Sauce.
5. Arrange potatoes near the rim of the dish. Loosely cover with foil and bake for 20 minutes or until scallops are tender and sauce is bubbly. Sprinkle remaining parsley on potato slices. Serve warm with crusty bread.
Yield: 4 servings
Suggested fish: bay scallops, cut into 1/2 pieces; 1-inch chunks of firm-flesh fish such as monk fish, halibut, catfish, swordfish, tuna, or salmon.
Per serving: 360 calories, 30 grams protein, 51 grams carbohydrate, 5 grams fat (12%), 51 milligrams cholesterol, 520 milligrams sodium.
